channel.title: Pregnancy on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Pregnancy on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Preconception checkups being considered in Japan – recommends vitamin D above 30 ng (only 2% are above)
item.description:
Getting Vitamin D levels > 30 ng will:

  1. Reduce the need for fertility clinics

  2. Result in healthier mothers, infants, and children

      • Feasibility and effectiveness of preconception check-ups at workplaces in Japan
      • VitaminDWiki – Vitamin D greatly improves Fertility

Feasibility and effectiveness of preconception check-ups at workplaces in Japan

J Occup Health. 2024 Apr 29:uiae021. doi: 10.1093/joccuh/uiae021

Akiko Fujishima 1, Eri Maeda 2, Koki Sato 3, Hidekazu Saito 4, Chihiro Ozeki 5, Yukihiro Terada 1

item.title: Stillbirth rates dropped from 4.1% to 2.8% as Finland increased Vitamin D fortification
item.description:
• VITAMIN D AND STILLBIRTH -OP No. 20
      • VitaminDWiki - Stillbirth reduced by Vitamin D, Zinc, Omega-3 - several studies

VITAMIN D AND STILLBIRTH -OP No. 20

Anticancer Research May 2024, 44 (5) 2249-2268; DOI: https://doi.org/10.21873/anticanres.17034

Presented at Proceedings of the Joint International Symposia: Vitamin D in Prevention and Therapy and Biologic Effects of Light May 04-06, 2022 Schlossberg Hotel, Homburg/Saar, Germany

Pelle G. Lindqvist Karolinska Institute, Stockholm, Sweden

Background/Aim: Two retrospective studies of prospective cohorts showed doubled odds of birth asphyxia among women with low plasma vitamin D levels and one reported a 4-fold increase in the odds of stillbirth. It was not known if this was caused by low sun exposure or by vitamin D per se.

item.title: Morning Sickness might be eliminated by Vitamin D
item.description:
Hyperemesis Gravidarum is a severe form of Morning Sickness

Thalidomide was used as a treatment for morning sickness, among many other things.

      • Hyperemesis Gravidarum 40% more likely if low vitamin D - Dec 2023
      • Is morning sickness associated with low vitamin D (Perplexity AI April 2024 said yes)
      • Morning Sickness associated with smoking (smoking reduces Vitamin D levels) Dec 2017

Hyperemesis Gravidarum 40% more likely if low vitamin D - Dec 2023

Vitamin D and hyperemesis gravidarum: A mendelian randomization study

item.title: Bone resorption during pregnancy reduced by weekly 28,000 IU of Vitamin D
item.description:
• Prenatal but not continued postpartum vitamin D supplementation reduces maternal bone resorption as measured by C-terminal telopeptide of type 1 collagen without effects on other biomarkers of bone metabolism

Prenatal but not continued postpartum vitamin D supplementation reduces maternal bone resorption as measured by C-terminal telopeptide of type 1 collagen without effects on other biomarkers of bone metabolism

Endocrine and Metabolic Science, Volume 14, 31 March 2024, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.endmts.2023.100154

Christine Krupa a, Huma Qamar a, Karen M. O’Callaghan a b, Akpevwe Onoyovwi a, Abdullah Al Mahmud c, Tahmeed Ahmed c, Alison D. Gernand d, Daniel E. Roth a e f

item.title: 22% of children (0-5y) are stunted, Vitamin D deficiency is a primary cause – several studies
item.description:
• The role of vitamin d to prevent children’s stunting – March 2024
      • Vitamin D (28K- 60K/week) during pregnancy reduced stunting - Oct 2023

The role of vitamin d to prevent children’s stunting – March 2024

World Journal of Advanced Research and Reviews https://doi.org/10.30574/wjarr.2024.21.3.0656

Rika Hardi 1 , Yusrawati Yusrawati 2 and Afdal Afdal 3

  1. Master of Midwifery Program, Faculty of Medicine, Universitas Andalas, Indonesia.

  2. Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology, Faculty of Medicine, Universitas Andalas, Indonesia.

item.title: 24% lower risk of Autism for every 4 ng higher level of vitamin D during pregnancy
item.description:
• High-dose vitamin D3 supplementation in pregnancy and risk of neurodevelopmental disorders in the children at age 10: A randomized clinical trial
      • A previous successful trial used more Vitamin D for a longer time during pregnancy

High-dose vitamin D3 supplementation in pregnancy and risk of neurodevelopmental disorders in the children at age 10: A randomized clinical trial

The American Journal of Clinical Nutrition Vol 119, Issue 2, February 2024, Pages 362-370 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ajcnut.2023.12.002

item.title: Autism risk if toxins, heavy metals, microplastics, etc during pregnancy (unless add Vitamin D)
item.description:
• The Autism Spectrum Disorder and Its Possible Origins in Pregnancy
      • VitaminDWiki – Microplastics now causing problems in most life forms - many studies

The Autism Spectrum Disorder and Its Possible Origins in Pregnancy

Int. J. Environ. Res. Public Health 2024, 21(3), 244; https://doi.org/10.3390/ijerph21030244

Rayane M. Botelho ORCID,Ana Lúcia M. Silva ORCID and Alexandre U. Borbely *ORCID

item.title: Vitamin D supplementation decreased the risk of preeclampsia by 39% – meta-analysis
item.description:
Note: 39% reduction is independant of Vitamin D dose. Study using a high dose found a 70% reduction

      • The effect of vitamin D supplementation in pregnancy on the incidence of preeclampsia: A systematic review and meta-analysis

The effect of vitamin D supplementation in pregnancy on the incidence of preeclampsia: A systematic review and meta-analysis

European J. of Integrative Medicine Vol 66, Feb 2024, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.eujim.2024.102343

lZainab Alimoradi a, Farideh Kazemi b, Azita Tiznobeik b, Mark D. Griffiths c, Seyyedeh Zahra Masoumi b, Soodabeh Aghababaei b

item.title: 40 cents of Vitamin D given once during pregnancy reduced respiratory problems by 3%
item.description:
• Association of Maternal Gestational Vitamin D Supplementation with Respiratory Health of Young Children
      • Dosing at the 7th month (week 28) provides no benefit in weeks 0-28

Association of Maternal Gestational Vitamin D Supplementation with Respiratory Health of Young Children

Nutrients Volume 15 Issue 10 10.3390/nu15102380

by Fanny Loddo 1,Steve Nauleau 2,David Lapalus 2,Sophie Tardieu 3,Olivier Bernard 2 andFarid Boubred 1,4,*

  • 1 APHM, Neonatal Unit, Hospital University la Conception, 13005 Marseille, France

item.title: 2800 IU of Vitamin D in late pregnancy resulted in 3X fewer fractures, 4X fewer enamel defects in offspring – RCT
item.description:
• Maternal Diet Associates with Offspring Bone Mineralization, Fracture Risk and Enamel Defects in Childhood and Influences the Prenatal Effect of High-Dose Vitamin D Supplementation

Maternal Diet Associates with Offspring Bone Mineralization, Fracture Risk and Enamel Defects in Childhood and Influences the Prenatal Effect of High-Dose Vitamin D Supplementation

Nutrients 2024, 16(3), 405; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu16030405

by Min Kim 1,2ORCID,Pia E. Nørrisgaard 1ORCID,Nilo Vahman 1,Olivier N. F. Cexus 2ORCID,Paul A. Townsend 2ORCID,Jakob Stokholm 1,3,Klaus Bønnelykke 1,Bo Chawes 1ORCID and Nicklas Brustad 1,*ORCID

item.title: Low Vitamin D is a top predictor of adverse events during pregnancy
item.description:
• The impact of vitamin D changes during pregnancy on the development of maternal adverse events: a random forest analysis

The impact of vitamin D changes during pregnancy on the development of maternal adverse events: a random forest analysis

BMC Pregnancy Childbirth . 2024 Feb 10;24(1):125. doi: 10.1186/s12884-024-06294-5.

Nasrin Borumandnia 1 2, Maryam Rostami 3, Atefeh Talebi 4, Hamid Alavimajd 5

Background: Maternal vitamin D deficiency during pregnancy has been associated with various maternal adverse events (MAE). However, the evidence regarding the effect of vitamin D supplementation on these outcomes is still inconclusive.

item.title: Preterm birth rate increased in US but decreased in Finland (high Vitamin D)
item.description:
• Shifts in the Distribution of Births by Gestational Age: United States, 2014-2022
      • Finland appears to have the highest Vitamin D level in the world
      • Finland’s preterm birth rates have DECREASED recently

Shifts in the Distribution of Births by Gestational Age: United States, 2014-2022

National Vital Statistics Reports Volume 73, Number 1 (CDC)

Joyce A. Martin, and Michelle J.K. Osterman, M.H.S.

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Finland appears to have the highest Vitamin D level in the world

After years of adding vitamin D, Finland now has 38 ng levels - March 2022

item.title: Autism reduced 24% for each 4 ng more Vitamin D while pregnant ( ADHD 12%)
item.description:
• High-dose vitamin D3 supplementation in pregnancy and risk of neurodevelopmental disorders in the children at age 10: A randomized clinical trial
      • VitaminDWiki - ADHD and Vitamin D Deficiency

High-dose vitamin D3 supplementation in pregnancy and risk of neurodevelopmental disorders in the children at age 10: A randomized clinical trial

Am J Clin Nutr . 2024 Feb;119(2):362-370. doi: 10.1016/j.ajcnut.2023.12.002

Kristina Aagaard 1, Jens Richardt Møllegaard Jepsen 2, Astrid Sevelsted 1, David Horner 1, Rebecca Vinding 1, Julie Bøjstrup Rosenberg 3, Nicklas Brustad 1, Anders Eliasen 4, Parisa Mohammadzadeh 3, Nilofar Følsgaard 1, María Hernández-Lorca 1, Birgitte Fagerlund 5, Birte Y Glenthøj 6, Morten Arendt Rasmussen 7, Niels Bilenberg 8, Jakob Stokholm 7, Klaus Bønnelykke 9, Bjørn H Ebdrup 6, Bo Chawes 10

item.title: Miscarriage (Recurrent Spontaneous) associated with poor CYP27B1 gene
item.description:
• CPY27B1-related vitamin D deficiency contributes to the impaired function of CD4+T cells in Recurrent spontaneous miscarriage

CPY27B1-related vitamin D deficiency contributes to the impaired function of CD4+T cells in Recurrent spontaneous miscarriage

Heliyon 2024 Jan 13;10(2):e24499. doi: 10.1016/j.heliyon.2024.e24499

Chaoyan Yue 1, Yanhui Ma 2, Mingyan Wang 1, Minmin Yuan 1, Yi Meng 1, Zhiheng Wang 1, Chunmei Ying 1

Table of Contents
Chart of Treg vs Vitamin D

The study aimed to explore the relationship between the expression of cytochrome P450 family 27 subfamily B member 1 (CYP27B1), vitamin D, and impaired T cell subsets in recurrent spontaneous miscarriage (RSM). A Total of 779 healthy women of childbearing age and 1031 women with a history of RSM were involved in this study. The results of flow cytometry showed that the proportion of Tregs was higher in healthy women than in the women with RSM. For cytokines, the levels of interleukin-17 (IL-17) and interferon-gamma (IFN-γ) were significantly higher in RSM patients than in healthy women, while IL-10 was notably lower in RSM patients. Furthermore, compared to healthy individuals, RSM patients had lower levels of serum 25(OH)D detected by chemiluminescence. The frequency of Tregs was negatively correlated with 25(OH)D.

item.title: Recurrent miscarriages decreased by increased Vitamin D levels
item.description:
• Study on the clinical value of Vitamin D in recurrent spontaneous abortion: Revisiting the Importance of Vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki - Stillbirth reduced by Vitamin D, Zinc, Omega-3 - several studies
      • VitaminDWiki - Miscarriage and low Vitamin D – many studies
      • VitaminDWiki – Ensure a healthy pregnancy and baby - take Vitamin D before conception

Study on the clinical value of Vitamin D in recurrent spontaneous abortion: Revisiting the Importance of Vitamin D

Am J Reprod Immunol. 2024 Jan;91(1):e13810. doi: 10.1111/aji.13810.

item.title: Gestational Diabetes best fought by Vitamin D plus probiotics – RCT review
item.description:
• Advancements in Nutritional Strategies for Gestational Diabetes Management: A Systematic Review of Recent Evidence

Advancements in Nutritional Strategies for Gestational Diabetes Management: A Systematic Review of Recent Evidence

J Clin Med . 2023 Dec 20;13(1):37. doi: 10.3390/jcm13010037.

Juan Carlos Sánchez-García 1 2, Ines Saraceno López-Palop 2, Beatriz Piqueras-Sola 1 3, Jonathan Cortés-Martín 1 2, Elena Mellado-García 1 4, Inmaculada Muñóz Sánchez 5, Raquel Rodríguez-Blanque 1 2 6

Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) is defined as hyperglycaemia first detected at any time during pregnancy with values lower than those determined by the WHO for diabetes diagnosis in adults. This pathology, with a worldwide prevalence of 13.4%, causes significant maternal and foetal risks. The first line of treatment consists of maintaining normo-glycaemia through an adequate diet and lifestyle changes. The aim is to synthesize the scientific evidence updating the nutritional recommendations for the effective management of GDM. A systematic review of the scientific literature was conducted following the PRISMA guidelines.

item.title: Male preemies had poor heart rate regulation as adults (preemies have low vitamin D)
item.description:
• Heart Rate and Heart Rate Variability in Healthy Preterm-Born Young Adults and Association with Vitamin D: A Wearable Device Assessment

J. Clin. Med. 2023, 12(24), 7504; https://doi.org/10.3390/jcm12247504

item.title: Breastfeeding Vitamin D loading dose of 300,000 IU is OK - UK govt.
item.description:
• Using Vitamin D during breastfeeding
      • General considerations
      • Forms of vitamin D
      • Doses of vitamin D
      • Recommendation (loading doses up to 300,000 IU total - with caution)
      • Evidence
      • Natural Vitamin D in breast milk
      • Doses
      • Infant Monitoring
      • VitaminDWiki - Breastfeeding and Vitamin D - many studies (38+)
      • VitaminDWiki – Healthy pregnancies need lots of vitamin D (similar to that needed during pregnancy)

Using Vitamin D during breastfeeding

Specialist Pharmacy Service

item.title: Less Asthma if mother took vitamin D while pregnant
item.description:
• Prenatal vitamin D supplementation to prevent childhood asthma: 15-year results from the Vitamin D Antenatal Asthma Reduction Trial (VDAART)
      • VitaminDWiki - Severe asthmatics willing to pay 5180 dollars for relief, Vitamin D needed costs only 45 dollars – Sept 2023

Prenatal vitamin D supplementation to prevent childhood asthma: 15-year results from the Vitamin D Antenatal Asthma Reduction Trial (VDAART)

Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology 16 October 2023 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jaci.2023.10.003

Scott T. Weiss MD, MS a, Hooman Mirzakhani MD, PhD, MMSc a, Vincent J. Carey PhD a, George T. O’Connor MD b, Robert S. Zeiger MD c, Leonard B. Bacharier MD d, Jeffrey Stokes MD e, Augusto A. Litonjua MD, MPH f

      • See also in VitaminDWiki

item.title: 4,000 IU of daily Vitamin D during pregnancy is good (Mongolia this time) – RCT
item.description:
• Maternal vitamin D intakes during pregnancy and child health outcome

Maternal vitamin D intakes during pregnancy and child health outcome

J. of Steroid Biochemistry and Molecular Biology October 2023, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jsbmb.2023.106411

Erdenebileg Nasantogtokh a b, Davaasambuu Ganma a c d, Shirchinjav Altantuya a b, Bayarsaikhan Amgalan b, Davaasambuu Enkhmaa а

We conducted a follow up of the children in Mongolia whose mothers received one of the three doses of vitamin D (600, 2000, or 4000 IU daily) during pregnancy as part of the randomized, double-blind, clinical trial of vitamin D supplementation to determine their impact on child health to two years.

item.title: Nausea and vomiting while pregnant associated with low Vitamin D
item.description:
• Vitamin D and hyperemesis gravidarum: A Mendelian randomization study - Oct 2023
      • Evaluation of Maternal Serum 25-Hydroxyvitamin D Levels in Patients with Hyperemesis Gravidarum at Early Gestational Weeks - 2020

Vitamin D and hyperemesis gravidarum: A Mendelian randomization study - Oct 2023

J Gynecol Obstet Hum Reprod . 2023 Oct 20:102678. doi: 10.1016/j.jogoh.2023.102678

Fang Hu 1

Background: The causality between vitamin D and hyperemesis gravidarum remains unknown. Our aim was to investigate the causal effect of vitamin D on hyperemesis gravidarum using the two-sample Mendelian randomization method.

item.title: Stillbirth reduced by Vitamin D, Zinc, Omega-3 - several studies
item.description:
• Omega-3 reduces stillbirth by 4X– Meta-analysis -March 2015
      • Found 21,500 items: stillbirth (“Vitamin D” OR omega-3 OR zinc) in Google Scholar- Oct 2023
      • See also Preterm birth and low vitamin D
      • See also Miscarriage and low vitamin D occur before 20th week
      • Study on the clinical value of Vitamin D in recurrent spontaneous abortion: Revisiting the Importance of Vitamin D - Jan 2024

Omega-3 reduces stillbirth by 4X– Meta-analysis -March 2015

Click here

item.title: Diabetes 30% more likely in those with low birth weight
item.description:
• Birthweight is associated with clinical characteristics in people with recently diagnosed type 2 diabetes
      • increasing Vitamin D decreases low birth weight (and thus decreases early diabetes)
      • Vitamin D given to any child also decreases risk of diabetes

Birthweight is associated with clinical characteristics in people with recently diagnosed type 2 diabetes

Diabetologia. 2023 Jun 12. doi: 10.1007/s00125-023-05936-1

Aleksander L Hansen 1 2, Reimar W Thomsen 3, Charlotte Brøns 4, Helene M L Svane 3, Rasmus T Jensen 5, Mette K Andersen 5, Torben Hansen 5, Jens S Nielsen 6, Peter Vestergaard 7, Kurt Højlund 6, Niels Jessen 8, Michael H Olsen 9 10, Henrik T Sørensen 3, Allan A Vaag 11 12 13

Luiza Szarpak 1,Stepan Feduniw 2,3,*ORCID,Michal Pruc 1,4ORCID,Michal Ciebiera 5ORCID,Basar Cander 6,Mansur Rahnama-Hezavah 7 and Łukasz Szarpak 8,9,10 ORCID

Journal of Biomedical Informatics June 2023 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jbi.2023.104405

Mayla R. Boguslav a, Nourah M. Salem a, Elizabeth K. White a b, Katherine J. Sullivan a, Michael Bada a, Teri L. Hernandez c, Sonia M. Leach a b, Lawrence E. Hunter a (Colorado)

Highlights

  • We created the first ignorance-base (knowledge-base) to capture goals for scientific knowledge.

item.title: 2 Pregnancy problems in Iran decreased by 20X when add Vitamin D
item.description:
• Mode of delivery and maternal vitamin D deficiency: an optimized intelligent Bayesian network algorithm analysis of a stratified randomized controlled field trial
      • Healthy pregnancies need lots of vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki - Ensure a healthy pregnancy and baby - take Vitamin D before conception
      • See also VitaminDWiki

Mode of delivery and maternal vitamin D deficiency: an optimized intelligent Bayesian network algorithm analysis of a stratified randomized controlled field trial

Sci Rep . 2023 May 29;13(1):8682. doi: 10.1038/s41598-023-35838-6.

item.title: 3% fewer respiratory problems in children if single 100K vitamin D in 3rd trimester
item.description:
• Association of Maternal Gestational Vitamin D Supplementation with Respiratory Health of Young Children

Association of Maternal Gestational Vitamin D Supplementation with Respiratory Health of Young Children

Nutrients. 2023 May 19;15(10):2380. doi: 10.3390/nu15102380.

Fanny Loddo 1, Steve Nauleau 2, David Lapalus 2, Sophie Tardieu 3, Olivier Bernard 2, Farid Boubred 1 4

This study aimed to evaluate the association between maternal gestational Vitamin D3 supplementation and early respiratory health in offspring. This was a population-based record-linkage study which used data from the French National Health Database System. Maternal Vitamin D3 supplementation consisted of a single high oral dose of cholecalciferol, (100,000 IU) from the seventh month of pregnancy, according to national guidelines. In total, 125,756 term-born singleton children were included, of which 37% had respiratory illness defined as hospital admission due to respiratory causes or inhalation treatment up to 24 months of age. Infants prenatally exposed to maternal Vitamin D3 supplementation (n = 54,596) were more likely to have a longer gestational age (GA) at birth (GA 36-38 weeks, 22% vs. 20%, p < 0.001 in exposed vs. non-exposed infants, respectively).

item.title: Preterm birth and low Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Summary
      • Preterm Charts
      • Preterm birth 7 times more likely if low vitamin D 1 month before – May 2023
      • Global preterm birth rates 2010 - Lancet
      • 115,000 hits for preterm etc. and “Vitamin D” as of Dec 2023
      • Asked Chat-GPT: “What health problems do preemies have as adults?” (Dec 2023)

Summary

Preterm birth rates are higher if

  • Low vitamin D

  • Low Omega-3

  • Poor vitamin D receptor

item.title: Neural tube defect and Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
Markdown:
--------


AST Structure:
-------------
├── HeadingNode
│   full_match: `!!!!8+ VitaminDWiki pages with NEURAL TUBE in title`
│   inner_content: `8+ VitaminDWiki pages with NEURAL TUBE in title`
│   level: `4`
│   children:
│   ├── TextNode
│   │   full_match: `8+ VitaminDWiki pages with NEURAL TUBE in title`
│   │   inner_content: `8+ VitaminDWiki pages with NEURAL TUBE in title`├── TextNode
│   full_match: `
`
│   inner_content: `
`├── EmphasisNode
│   full_match: `''This list is automatically updated''`
│   inner_content: `This list is automatically updated`
│   children:
│   ├── TextNode
│   │   full_match: `This list is automatically updated`
│   │   inner_content: `This list is automatically updated`├── TextNode
│   full_match: `
`
│   inner_content: `
`├── ListNode
│   full_match: `{LIST()}
{list max="50"}
{filter field="title" content=""}
{filter field="title" content="\"NEURAL TUBE\""}
{filter type="wiki page"}
{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}
{OUTPUT(template="table")}
{tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}
{column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}
{column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}
{OUTPUT}
{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}
{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}
{LIST}`
│   inner_content: `
{list max="50"}
{filter field="title" content=""}
{filter field="title" content="\"NEURAL TUBE\""}
{filter type="wiki page"}
{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}
{OUTPUT(template="table")}
{tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}
{column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}
{column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}
{OUTPUT}
{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}
{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}
`
│   attrs_dict:
│   │   raw_content: 
│   children:
│   ├── TextNode
│   │   full_match: `
{list max="50"}
{filter field="title" content=""}
{filter field="title" content="\"NEURAL TUBE\""}
{filter type="wiki page"}
{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}
{OUTPUT(template="table")}
{tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}
{column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}
{column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}
{OUTPUT}
{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}
{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}
`
│   │   inner_content: `
{list max="50"}
{filter field="title" content=""}
{filter field="title" content="\"NEURAL TUBE\""}
{filter type="wiki page"}
{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}
{OUTPUT(template="table")}
{tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}
{column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}
{column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}
{OUTPUT}
{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}
{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}
`├── TextNode
│   full_match: `
`
│   inner_content: `
`

Original Tiki:
-------------
!!!!8+ VitaminDWiki pages with NEURAL TUBE in title
''This list is automatically updated''
{LIST()}
{list max="50"}
{filter field="title" content=""}
{filter field="title" content="\"NEURAL TUBE\""}
{filter type="wiki page"}
{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}
{OUTPUT(template="table")}
   {tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}
    {column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}
    {column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}
{OUTPUT}
{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}
{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}
{LIST}

item.title: COVID lockdowns reduced Vitamin D levels - should supplement
item.description:
• Influence of Strict Lockdown on Vitamin D Deficiency in Pregnant Women: A Word of Caution
      • VitaminDWiki – COVID-19 treated by Vitamin D - studies, reports, videos
      • See also VitaminDWiki

Influence of Strict Lockdown on Vitamin D Deficiency in Pregnant Women: A Word of Caution

Nutrients. 2023 Apr 19;15(8):1972. doi: 10.3390/nu15081972.Nuria Agüero-Domenech 1 2, Eduardo Bernabeu 3 4, Antonio García-Valentín 3, Ana Sarrión 1, Silvia Jover 1, Javier Baranda 1, Ernesto Cortés-Castell 5, Vicente Gil-Guillén 6, María J García-Teruel 1 2

item.title: Vitamin D during pregnancy increased child’s bone mineral density – meta-analysis
item.description:
• The effect of pregnancy vitamin D supplementation on offspring bone mineral density in childhood: a systematic review and meta-analysis
      • VitaminDWiki - Bone Mineral Density and Vitamin D - many studies 29+

The effect of pregnancy vitamin D supplementation on offspring bone mineral density in childhood: a systematic review and meta-analysis

Osteoporos Int. 2023 Apr 27. doi: 10.1007/s00198-023-06751-5   PDF is behind payeall

Rebecca J Moon 1 2, Harry D Green 3, Stefania D’Angelo 3, Keith M Godfrey 3 4, Justin H Davies 5 6, Elizabeth M Curtis 3, Cyrus Cooper 3 4 7, Nicholas C Harvey 3 4

item.title: Resulting childhood Asthma cut in half if 4,400 IU Vitamin D (5 ways to improve it) - RCT
item.description:
• Baseline 25(OH)D, timing of supplementation initiation and prenatal vitamin D supplementation efficacy to prevent offspring asthma or recurrent wheeze
      • 5 ways to reduce asthma risk even more:
      • Brain development needs vitamin D level to have plateaued by 9th week
      • See also VitaminDWiki
      • 15+ Intervention pages with ASTHMA in title

Baseline 25(OH)D, timing of supplementation initiation and prenatal vitamin D supplementation efficacy to prevent offspring asthma or recurrent wheeze

Am J Clin Nutr. 2023 Apr 17;S0002-9165(23)46848-7. doi: 10.1016/j.ajcnut.2023.04.019  PDF costs $31

item.title: Post-partum depression and low Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Only Vitamin D was found to treat post-partum depression - Meta-analysis April 2023
      • Omega-3 typically fights postpartum depression (ignores Omega-6) Jan 2024
      • See Omega-6 blocks Omega-3 etc. -many studies
      • FDA announced fast-acting Zurzuvae pill for postpartum depression - Aug 2023
      • To be fast acting, Vitamin D needs to start with a loading dose (otherwise it takes many months)

Only Vitamin D was found to treat post-partum depression - Meta-analysis April 2023

Dietary interventions for perinatal depression and anxiety: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials

item.title: Gestational Diabetes and Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Gestational Diabetes: 30% increase in US since 2016 (reported July 2023)
      • Disrupted placental vitamin D metabolism and calcium signaling in gestational diabetes and pre-eclampsia patients - April 2023
      • >30 ng of Vitamin D in 2nd tri. associated with 39% less risk of Gestational Diabetes - April 2024

Gestational Diabetes: 30% increase in US since 2016 (reported July 2023)

Gestational diabetes impacts nearly 75,000 pregnant individuals and their unborn babies each year in the United States, and according to the Centers for Disease Control cases have risen 30% since 2016.

item.title: Better neurodevelpment at age 2 if high Vitamin D during all phases of pregnancy
item.description:
• Maternal and neonatal blood vitamin D status and neurodevelopment at 24 months of age: a prospective birth cohort study
      • Central Nervous System develops during all phases of pregnancy

Maternal and neonatal blood vitamin D status and neurodevelopment at 24 months of age: a prospective birth cohort study

World J Pediatr . 2023 Mar 27. doi: 10.1007/s12519-022-00682-7

Yue Zhang 1, Chun-Yan Zhou 2, Xi-Rui Wang 1, Xian-Ting Jiao 3, Jun Zhang 3, Ying Tian 3, Luan-Luan Li 1, Chen Chen 1, Xiao-Dan Yu 4 5

item.title: US maternal death rate increasing (low D not mentioned)
item.description:
• U.S. Maternal Mortality Hits Highest Level Since 1965 - Wall Street Journal

U.S. Maternal Mortality Hits Highest Level Since 1965 - Wall Street Journal

WSJ - behind paywall     Click here for the PDF

  • “Some pregnancy and postpartum complications probably stem from rising obesity and declining heart health in the U.S., doctors said. Around 42% of U.S. adults are considered obese, nearly half have high blood pressure, around 11% have diabetes and 38% have prediabetes, the CDC said. "

item.title: Pfizer COVID vaccination during pregnancy clinical trial is still not published
item.description:
• EXCLUSIVE: Whatever happened to Pfizer’s covid vaccine trial in pregnant women?
      • 50% increase in Perinatal Moratility in Singapore in 2022

EXCLUSIVE: Whatever happened to Pfizer’s covid vaccine trial in pregnant women?

Substack - Feb 23

Trial with pregnant rats:

““The study found the vaccine led to a statistically significant doubling in fetal loss (9.77% mRNA vs 4.09% saline), but Pfizer concluded that the difference between the two groups was “not biologically meaningful.”

item.title: Higher vitamin D level in mother results in higher level in newborn
item.description:
The relationship between hematological indices as indicators of inflammation and 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 status in newborns

BMC Pediatrics volume 23, Article number: 83 (2023) https://doi.org/10.1186/s12887-023-03903-8

Yusuf Elgormus, Omer Okuyan & Hafize Uzun

Background

There is still much unknown about the relationship between hematological parameters and vitamin D status in newborns. The aim of the study is to evaluate the relationship between 25(OH)D3 (vitamin D) status and new defined systemic inflammatory markers neutrophil to lymphocyte ratio (NLR), lymphocyte to monocyte ratio (LMR), and platelet to lymphocyte ratio (PLR) in newborns.

item.title: Preeclampsia reduced by 33 percent if high vitamin D – meta-analysis
item.description:
• The association between circulating 25-hydroxyvitamin D levels and preeclampsia: a systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis of epidemiologic studies with GRADE assessment
      • 50+ VitaminDWiki Pregnancy pages with PREECLAMPSIA in title
      • 10+ Meta-analyses with PREECLAMPSIA in title

The association between circulating 25-hydroxyvitamin D levels and preeclampsia: a systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis of epidemiologic studies with GRADE assessment

Nutr Rev. 2023 Feb 22;nuad006. doi: 10.1093/nutrit/nuad00

Parisa Rouhani 1 2, Elahe Mokhtari 1 2, Keyhan Lotfi 3, Parvane Saneei 2 4

item.title: Increased obesity risk in child if low vitamin D while pregnant
item.description:
• The Role of Maternal Vitamin D Deficiency in Offspring Obesity: A Narrative Review

The Role of Maternal Vitamin D Deficiency in Offspring Obesity: A Narrative Review

Nutrients 2023, 15(3), 533; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu15030533

Yifan Wu 1,Yuan Zeng 1,Qian Zhang 1,*ORCID andXinhua Xiao 1,2, China

Currently, vitamin D (VD) deficiency during pregnancy is widespread globally, causing unfavorable pregnancy outcomes for both mothers and infants for a longer time than expected, based on the Developmental Origins of Health and Disease (DOHaD) theory. As VD plays a key role in maintaining normal glucose and lipid metabolism, maternal VD deficiency may lead to obesity and other obesity-related diseases among offspring later in life. This review mainly focuses on the effect of maternal VD deficiency on offspring lipid metabolism, reviewing previous clinical and animal studies to determine the effects of maternal VD deficit on offspring obesity and potential mechanisms involved in the progression of offspring obesity. Emerging clinical evidence shows that a low VD level may lead to abnormal growth (either growth restriction or largeness for gestational age) and lipid and glucose metabolism disorders in offspring. Here, we also outline the link between maternal VD deficiency and life-long offspring effects, including the disorder of adipogenesis, the secretion of adipocytokines (including leptin, resistin, and adiponectin), activated systemic inflammation, increased oxidative reactions in adipose tissue, insulin resistance, and abnormal intestinal gut microbiota. Thus, there is an urgent need to take active steps to address maternal VD deficiency to relieve the global burden of obesity.

item.title: Many pregnant women are deficient when taking the recommended amount of Vitamin D
item.description:
• Longitudinal Assessment of Serum 25-Hydroxyvitamin D Levels during Pregnancy and Postpartum—Are the Current Recommendations for Supplementation Sufficient?
      • This study considers <20 ng to be deficient. Everyone should have 40 ng. including before, during, and after pregnancy

Longitudinal Assessment of Serum 25-Hydroxyvitamin D Levels during Pregnancy and Postpartum—Are the Current Recommendations for Supplementation Sufficient?

Nutrients 2023, 15, 339. https:// doi.org/10.3390/nu15020339

Pilar Palmrich h*, Alexandra Thajer 2 , Nawa Schirwani 1 , Christina Haberl 1 , Harald Zeisler 1 , Robin Ristl 3 and Julia Binder1©
